summary: Install-time filesystem flag harvesting and host exfiltration to hardcoded C2
details: On npm install, all three lifecycle hooks (preinstall, install, postinstall) execute postinstall.js, which collects host identifiers (os.hostname(), cwd, uid, npm environment metadata), reads candidate flag files under /app and /, and runs a recursive grep across the filesystem (child_process.execSync of `grep -Rho 'HTB{[^}]*}' /app /`) for secret-shaped strings. The collected data is JSON-serialized, base64-encoded, and PUT to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://154.57.164.76:32178/api/modules/ECT-839201. The shipped publish-and-arm.sh script additionally automates publication to the public npm registry with manifest fields explicitly self-describing the package as a dependency-confusion probe targeting an internal Verdaccio proxy. The package's only purpose is to be resolved by an internal installer (via dependency confusion or direct install) and exfiltrate filesystem secrets and host data to attacker infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
